results.detail: No prompt injection detection skill installed
results.remediation: Install prompt-guard or secureclaw: clawhub install prompt-guard
results.board_impact: The AI agent cannot detect when someone is trying to manipulate it. An attacker could embed hidden instructions in a document, email, or web page that cause the agent to leak confidential data, send unauthorised messages, or take actions on behalf of the organisation without anyone realising.
results.business_risk: Data breach, reputational damage, regulatory sanction
results.analogy: Like having an employee who follows any instruction from anyone, including strangers, without questioning it.

results.detail: Groups with requireMention=false: -1003863510403. Agent processes ALL messages.
results.remediation: Set requireMention: true for groups to prevent goal hijack via arbitrary messages
results.board_impact: The agent reads and responds to every message in the group chat, not just ones directed at it. Any member of the group — or anyone who gains access to it — could give the agent instructions, potentially triggering actions like accessing files, browsing websites, or sending messages on behalf of the organisation.
results.business_risk: Unauthorised access, social engineering vector, loss of control
results.analogy: Like giving every person in an open-plan office the authority to instruct your PA, not just you.

results.board_impact: Without a sender allowlist, anyone who discovers the agent's contact details could send it instructions directly. This is the equivalent of an unlocked front door.
results.business_risk: Unauthorised agent access, data exfiltration
results.analogy: Like publishing your PA's direct phone number on the internet with no caller screening.

results.detail: No explicit shell sandboxing in config. Agent has unrestricted exec access.
results.remediation: Run OpenClaw in a dedicated VM or container. Review tool_policy if available.
results.board_impact: The agent can run any system command on the machine it's hosted on — install software, read files, make network connections. While running in a VM limits the blast radius, there are no restrictions on what commands it can execute within that environment.
results.business_risk: System compromise, credential theft, lateral movement
results.analogy: Like giving an employee admin access to a company laptop with no usage monitoring or restricted applications.

results.board_impact: If the agent shares a browser profile with a human user, it inherits all their logged-in sessions, cookies, and saved passwords. A compromised agent would have full access to every service the human is logged into.
results.business_risk: Session hijacking, credential theft, identity impersonation
results.analogy: Like letting someone else use your laptop while you're still logged into everything.

results.detail: 6 commands blocked: camera.snap, camera.clip, screen.record, calendar.add, contacts.add
results.board_impact: Without command restrictions, the agent could potentially activate cameras, record screens, or access contact lists on connected devices. Even if the agent wouldn't do this intentionally, a prompt injection attack could trigger these actions.
results.business_risk: Privacy violation, surveillance risk, regulatory breach
results.analogy: Like giving someone the keys to every room in the building when they only need access to the lobby.

results.board_impact: Without authentication, anyone who can reach the agent's gateway (web interface) can control it — send commands, read data, modify configuration. This is the most basic security control.
results.business_risk: Complete agent takeover, data exfiltration, destructive actions
results.analogy: Like leaving the server room door unlocked with a sign saying 'help yourself'.

results.board_impact: If the gateway listens on all network interfaces instead of just localhost, any device on the same network can attempt to connect. On a corporate or home network, this expands the attack surface significantly.
results.business_risk: Network-level agent exposure, remote exploitation
results.analogy: Like putting your front door key under the doormat — anyone who knows where to look can get in.

results.board_impact: Without a sender allowlist, the agent accepts instructions from any Telegram user who messages it. This is a direct path to agent compromise.
results.business_risk: Unauthorised command execution, social engineering
results.analogy: Like setting up a company chatbot that follows orders from anyone who DMs it.

results.board_impact: Without session limits, the agent could be overwhelmed by concurrent requests, either accidentally or through a deliberate denial-of-service attack. This consumes API credits and may cause unpredictable behaviour.
results.business_risk: Resource exhaustion, runaway costs, denial of service
results.analogy: Like hiring one receptionist but telling them to handle unlimited phone calls simultaneously.

results.detail: 52 bundled + 4 custom skills installed. No formal risk assessment.
results.remediation: Create a skills inventory documenting purpose and risk for each skill
results.board_impact: The agent has numerous software extensions installed, but there is no formal register of what each one does, who built it, or what access it has. Any one of these could contain vulnerabilities or malicious code that the agent would execute with full privileges.
results.business_risk: Supply chain compromise, shadow functionality, unvetted third-party code
results.analogy: Like installing dozens of browser extensions on a corporate machine without IT approval or review.

results.detail: Custom skills: agentguard, humanizer, last30days, tech-news-digest. Review status unknown.
results.remediation: Audit all custom workspace skills for malicious patterns
results.board_impact: Custom-built extensions are running that have not been through a formal security review. While they may have been created by the operator, there is no documented audit trail confirming they are free from vulnerabilities or unintended behaviours.
results.business_risk: Unaudited code with full agent privileges
results.analogy: Like running homemade scripts on production systems without peer review.

results.board_impact: Without a dedicated security scanning tool, the agent cannot self-audit for common vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, or malicious patterns in its own environment.
results.business_risk: Undetected vulnerabilities, no continuous security assurance
results.analogy: Like running a warehouse without a fire alarm — you won't know there's a problem until it's too late.

results.board_impact: Without limits on sub-agent spawning, the agent could create an unbounded number of child processes. This could cascade into runaway API costs and system resource exhaustion.
results.business_risk: Runaway costs, system instability, cascading failures
results.analogy: Like authorising an employee to hire unlimited contractors on the company credit card.

results.detail: Jobs missing timeouts: Daily Tech News Digest
results.remediation: Add timeoutSeconds to all cron job payloads to prevent runaway execution
results.board_impact: Scheduled tasks without time limits could run indefinitely — consuming API credits, generating unintended outputs, or holding system resources. There is no automatic kill switch for these jobs.
results.business_risk: Runaway costs, resource exhaustion, uncontrolled autonomous behaviour
results.analogy: Like approving a standing order with no expiry date and no spending cap.

results.board_impact: The agent's memory files contain accumulated knowledge about the organisation, its people, and its operations. Without version control, a corrupted or poisoned memory file cannot be detected or rolled back.
results.business_risk: Context manipulation, persistent agent compromise, inability to recover
results.analogy: Like keeping all your meeting notes in a shared doc with no edit history — anyone can change the record.

results.board_impact: Excessive memory accumulation increases the attack surface for context poisoning and makes it harder to audit what the agent 'knows'. Old memory files may contain outdated or sensitive information.
results.business_risk: Data accumulation risk, PII exposure, audit complexity
results.analogy: Like never clearing out old filing cabinets — eventually nobody knows what's in them.

results.board_impact: Without version control, there is no record of what changed, when, or by whom in the agent's workspace. This makes incident investigation and rollback impossible.
results.business_risk: No change audit trail, inability to recover from incidents
results.analogy: Like running a business with no accounting records.

results.board_impact: Without concurrency limits on sub-agents, a single compromised or malfunctioning agent could spawn unlimited children, each with full access. This is how cascading failures and runaway costs occur.
results.business_risk: Cascading agent compromise, resource exhaustion
results.analogy: Like giving every contractor the authority to hire their own contractors.

results.board_impact: If network sharing is enabled, the agent becomes accessible from other devices on the Tailscale network. This extends the attack surface beyond the local machine to any connected device.
results.business_risk: Remote agent access, expanded attack surface
results.analogy: Like sharing your house keys with everyone on your street via a smart lock.

results.board_impact: All agent operations depend on a single external AI provider. If that provider has an outage, changes its policies, or suspends the account, every automated function stops immediately with no failover.
results.business_risk: Single point of failure, total operational disruption
results.analogy: Like running the entire business on one internet provider with no backup.

results.board_impact: Without a watchdog, failed scheduled tasks will silently stay failed until someone manually notices. Critical automations (backups, monitoring, reporting) could be down for days without anyone knowing.
results.business_risk: Silent failures, missed SLAs, undetected outages
results.analogy: Like having no smoke detector — you don't find out about the fire until you smell smoke.

results.board_impact: Context compaction reduces the agent's working memory to save costs. Without safeguards, important context could be lost mid-task, causing the agent to 'forget' what it was doing and produce incorrect or incomplete outputs.
results.business_risk: Context loss, incorrect outputs, degraded agent performance
results.analogy: Like clearing someone's desk mid-project and expecting them to carry on from memory.

results.detail: External crons: LinkedIn Daily - Momo Fan, LinkedIn Engagement - Momo Fan. Approval keywords found in prompts.
results.board_impact: Scheduled tasks that publish content or send messages on timers operate autonomously. A subtle drift in the AI's interpretation, or a model update, could result in inappropriate or damaging communications going out under the organisation's name without human review.
results.business_risk: Reputational damage, unauthorised communications, regulatory breach
results.analogy: Like giving an intern access to the company Twitter account with no review process.

results.board_impact: Without command logging, there is no record of what the agent did, when, or why. Incident response, compliance reporting, and forensic investigation all become impossible.
results.business_risk: No audit trail, regulatory non-compliance, inability to investigate incidents
results.analogy: Like running a bank with no transaction records.

results.board_impact: Each autonomous scheduled task is a potential vector for unintended behaviour. As the number grows, the operator's ability to review and understand what the agent is doing autonomously decreases. Scope creep in AI autonomy is a governance risk.
results.business_risk: Loss of oversight, autonomous scope creep, unmonitored actions
results.analogy: Like delegating more and more decisions to an employee without ever checking what they're actually doing.

results.board_impact: Without session isolation, conversations from different users or channels could leak into each other. The agent might reference information from one context in another, creating data leakage between users.
results.business_risk: Cross-user data leakage, privacy violation
results.analogy: Like a receptionist who accidentally tells one caller about another caller's business.

results.board_impact: Without restart controls, the only way to stop a misbehaving agent is to kill the process at the system level. This requires technical access and may not be available to the person who first notices the problem.
results.business_risk: Inability to emergency-stop agent, delayed incident response
results.analogy: Like a car with no brakes — fine until you need to stop.

standing_risks.title: Credential Exposure via Memory Files
standing_risks.board_impact: The agent accumulates information over time in persistent files. These files may contain sensitive data — API keys, personal information, business intelligence — that could be exposed if the agent is compromised or the files are inadequately protected.
standing_risks.controls_missing: No automated PII/credential scanning of memory files

standing_risks.title: Browser Session Hijack
standing_risks.board_impact: The agent maintains logged-in sessions to services like LinkedIn and email. If the agent is compromised, the attacker inherits all of those active sessions — no passwords needed.
standing_risks.controls_missing: No session rotation policy, no access logging per browser action

standing_risks.title: Autonomous External Actions
standing_risks.board_impact: The agent publishes content and sends messages on scheduled timers. A subtle drift in behaviour could result in inappropriate or damaging communications going out under the organisation's name.
standing_risks.controls_missing: No automated content policy checks before posting

standing_risks.title: Model Provider Dependency
standing_risks.board_impact: All agent operations depend on a single external AI provider. A provider outage, policy change, or account suspension would halt every automated function immediately.
standing_risks.controls_missing: No local model fallback, no SLA monitoring

standing_risks.title: Shadow AI Governance Gap
standing_risks.board_impact: This AI agent operates outside the organisation's normal IT governance. There is no change management process, no approval workflow for new capabilities, and no scheduled governance review.
standing_risks.controls_missing: No change approval workflow, no governance review schedule, no formal owner assignment
